violations.text: Raw animal foods (such as meats and seafood) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as sauces and cooked foods) in the 2 door refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. PIC reorganized fridge. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/22/2025

Beef birria, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. PIC disposed of item. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/22/2025

Cheese was observed held at 51 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC submerged container in ice water. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/22/2025

Inspector observed containers of various food items with discard dates well into the future (some greater than two weeks). Inspector reviewed date marking with PIC and PIC corrected discard dates. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/22/2025

Inspector observed in-use utensils being stored in a container of sanitizer between uses. PIC removed items to be washed/sanitized. PIC informed to end practice. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/22/2025

Inspector observed containers of beef birria cooling in 2 door cooler with lids and stacked. Lids were observed with condensation. PIC discarded. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/22/2025

The doors to the outside, located in the front and back, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/16/2025
Inspector observed food equipment (large pots) stored on the floor in the kitchen. Per interview, PIC has racks ordered and is awaiting arrival. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/10/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the refrigeration units (outside and inside) have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/22/2025
Menus lacks an allergy statement. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/14/2025

Dishes and utensils were observed in the hand-washing sinks (kitchen and bar). A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. PIC removed items. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/10/2025

Raw animal foods (such as eggs, shrimp, and octopus) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as cooked foods and sauces) in the two door refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. PIC relocated items. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/10/2025

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the bar dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. PIC called for service and service arrived during inspection. Unit is operating at 100 ppm after service. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/10/2025

White, yellow rice, and beans were observed held between 74-126 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC reheated. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/10/2025

Cheese and dice tomatoes was observed held between 55-57 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC placed items in fridge for active cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/10/2025

Various items were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 12/10/2025
The consumer advisory provided does not disclose the foods on the menu that are raw or undercooked. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/14/2025
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. New Violation. Correct By: 12/10/2025

Inspector observed several types of fish in ROP packaging, which were thawed and located in the cold hold drawer. PIC discarded ROP fish items that were above 38F. Items at or below 38F were opened and removed from packaging. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/10/2025

Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 12/10/2025

The doors to the outside, located in the front and back, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/16/2025
Various food items were observed stored on the floor in the kitchen area. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 12/10/2025
Utensils were observed stored in between table and prep unit between uses. PIC placed items in 3 bay sink to be washed and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/10/2025
Inspector observed clean equipment stored on the floor in the kitchen. Items must be stored at least 6 inches off the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 12/10/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of ALL refrigeration and freezer units (inside and outside) have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 12/10/2025

The ice machine is lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/15/2025

The floors and walls throughout the kitchen have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/10/2025
The menu is lacking an allergy statement. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 10/14/2025
The consumer advisory provided does not disclose the foods on the menu that are raw or undercooked. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 10/14/2025

The presence of flies are not controlled as evidenced by live activity. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 10/14/2025

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 10/16/2025

The ice machine is lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. New Violation. Correct By: 10/15/2025

The basement storage room has an accumulation of flies, clutter and other debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 10/17/2025
